subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Model Mix in Practice
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Model Mix in Practice.

hook.text: No real company uses just one service model. Modern stacks mix IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, and serverless together.

buildup.text: The database might be managed PaaS. The frontend deploys on serverless. The ML pipeline runs on IaaS VMs.

discovery.text: Choosing the right model per component — not per company — is how experienced teams architect systems.

twist.text: The mix evolves over time. Teams often start with PaaS, then move critical parts to IaaS for control.

climax.text: There's no perfect architecture. There's only the right architecture for your team, today.

punchline.text: Mix models per problem. There's no one-size-fits-all cloud.

quiz.question: How do experienced teams choose cloud service models?
options: They pick the best model for each individual component, not one model for everything
They always use the cheapest model for all components
They use only one model to keep the architecture simple
